skill.name: developer-experience
skill.description: Improves developer setup, onboarding, project scripts, git hooks, editor configuration, and workflow documentation.
skill.summary: Optimizes developer workflows with setup guides, automation ideas, tooling configuration, and onboarding templates.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, environment, network, and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown examples and onboarding placeholders, not executable skill code, but the skill does guide users to create shell scripts, aliases, hooks, and cleanup commands. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so the skill is publishable with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Executable Development Automation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The command detections are documentation examples, not code executed by the skill. The skill still guides users to create setup scripts, package scripts, git hooks, Makefile targets, and shell tasks, so generated commands require review before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is explicit Markdown guidance for shell and task-runner commands. It is not malicious code, but users may execute generated commands in local projects.

medium_findings.title: Shell Profile Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill suggests adding aliases to hidden shell profile files. This is a legitimate workflow improvement, but persistent shell configuration changes can alter command behavior across sessions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines name shell startup files and alias definitions directly. The risk is moderate because the example is simple and user-visible.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Cleanup Command Example
medium_findings.description: The Makefile example includes a cleanup target that removes dependency and build directories. This is common developer tooling, but recursive delete commands can cause data loss if copied into the wrong path or expanded without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static signal is semantically confirmed by a documented recursive delete command. The command targets common build directories, so it is risky but not malicious.

low_findings.title: Environment File Copy Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged environment file access. The files only show copying an example environment file into a local .env file, with no evidence of secret reading, credential harvesting, or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a standard onboarding setup step. No evidence found of reading secret values or sending them anywhere.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a hardcoded URL. The referenced line is a localhost placeholder for a development server, not an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly localhost with a template port. No evidence found of external network access or data transfer.

low_findings.title: Sensitive System and Weak Crypto False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Windows SAM and weak-cryptography indicators. Manual review found only generic template placeholders and unrelated words in documentation, with no code that accesses credential stores or implements cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are template text or ordinary prose. No evidence found of credential-store access, hashing functions, or cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Shell Commands Need Review
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed to produce shell scripts, git hooks, task-runner commands, and editor shell tasks. This is aligned with its purpose, but commands should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple examples explicitly include bash, shell, Makefile, and VS Code shell task content. The pattern is intentional and legitimate for this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Delete Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A cleanup target demonstrates recursive deletion of generated directories. This should remain scoped to project build artifacts and should not be generalized.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command appears in a Makefile example. The target directories are common artifacts, but recursive deletion is inherently sensitive.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when setup steps, scripts, and workflow rules are scattered or unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code organize onboarding, automation, and tooling improvements.
actual_capabilities: Reviews development workflows and identifies friction points.
Drafts setup scripts, package scripts, Makefile targets, and git hook ideas.
Creates onboarding guide content from a structured template.
Suggests editor, linter, formatter, and task-runner configuration.
Defines success metrics such as setup time, manual steps, and feedback speed.
limitations: Generated shell commands must be reviewed before execution.
It does not verify every dependency version or project-specific environment requirement.
It cannot guarantee that suggested automation is safe for all operating systems.
It provides documentation and configuration guidance, not runtime monitoring.

use_cases.title: Standardize New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Create a clear setup flow with prerequisites, install commands, environment steps, and verification checks.

use_cases.title: Reduce Repetitive Local Tasks
use_cases.description: Convert common build, test, lint, format, migration, and seed tasks into discoverable commands.

use_cases.title: Build Onboarding Documentation
use_cases.description: Prepare day-by-day onboarding notes, key file explanations, workflow rules, troubleshooting steps, and team contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Find Setup Friction
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project setup process and list the top friction points for a new developer.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit of an existing repository

prompt_templates.title: Create Common Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest safe package scripts or Makefile targets for install, dev, test, lint, format, database setup, and cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an Onboarding Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a developer onboarding guide with prerequisites, setup steps, verification checks, key files, first tasks, and troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize End-to-End DX
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the developer experience for this repository and propose changes to setup, feedback loops, editor configuration, git hooks, and documentation. Include risks for each command.

output_examples.input: Review our onboarding flow for a Node project.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of setup gaps, missing validation checks, clearer environment variable notes, and a shorter first-run path.

output_examples.input: Create shortcuts for repeated local development tasks.
output: A command list for development, testing, linting, formatting, database setup, and cleanup.
Notes about which commands can modify files or remove generated artifacts.

output_examples.input: Prepare onboarding documentation for a new backend engineer.
output_examples.output: A structured onboarding guide with prerequisites, clone and install steps, local verification, architecture notes, starter tasks, and troubleshooting.

best_practices: Ask the skill to explain the purpose and risk of each generated command.
Review shell scripts, cleanup targets, and profile changes before running them.
Keep generated onboarding guides aligned with the current repository and team process.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated setup or cleanup commands without reading them.
Do not copy aliases into shell profile files unless they are project-appropriate.
Do not treat placeholder onboarding values as verified project facts.

faq.question: Can this skill generate setup scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft setup scripts and commands, but users should review them before execution.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands by itself?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill executes commands. The reviewed files contain Markdown guidance and examples.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally produces shell commands, profile changes, and cleanup tasks. These are useful but require review.

faq.question: Can it help with developer onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a structured onboarding guide template for setup, orientation, first tasks, and troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the repository structure, existing scripts, setup instructions, known pain points, and operating system constraints.
